title: Crunchy British Lentils Croquettes
description: Sweet, crispy, proteinrich lentil croquettes featuring brown lentils, a goldensugar glaze, and a splash of dry English ale for a quintessential British twist.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Line a baking tray with parchment.
In a medium saucepan, bring the vegetable broth to a simmer. Add lentils, reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes until tender. Drain any excess liquid.
In a small saucepan, sauté onion and garlic in a tsp of oil until translucent, about 5 minutes. Add cumin and smoked paprika; cook for 1 minute.
Stir in brown sugar, golden syrup, and ale; let it reduce to a thick glaze (about 2–3 minutes). Then add the cooked lentils and mix until well coated. Season with salt and pepper.

Transfer the mixture to a shallow dish to cool slightly. While cooling, whisk the flour with a splash of water to make a thick paste; stir into the lentil mixture until thickened; let it chill in the fridge for 30 minutes.
Once chilled, shape the mixture into bite‑sized balls (about 2 cm diameter). Dredge each ball lightly in flour, then dip in beaten egg, and coat with panko breadcrumbs.
Heat 2 L of oil in a deep skillet over medium‑high heat. Fry the croquettes in batches until golden and crisp, about 3–4 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels.
In a small saucepan, combine the remaining glaze and a dash of additional ale if desired, and heat until glossy.
Drizzle the glaze over the hot croquettes or serve on the side as a dipping sauce. Serve immediately with a fresh green salad or a dash of mustard for extra zing.
